literal: a topical treatise, a section; from pra- + √kṛ 'to make'
invariant_definition: A topically-organized stand-alone treatise (as distinct from a *bhāṣya*'s text-following structure). *Prakaraṇa-grantha*s are concise expositions of school doctrine designed for direct study (as opposed to sūtra-following exposition). Important examples: *Vivekacūḍāmaṇi*, *Upadeśa-Sāhasrī*, *Vedānta-Sāra*, *Vedānta-Paribhāṣā*, *Tattva-Saṅkhyāna*, *Yatīndra-Mata-Dīpikā*.

per_school.definition: *Prakaraṇa-grantha*s function as introductory or systematic-overview texts for each school.
